reason: Response A is more restrained and calibrated to a coworker relationship, with simpler phrasing and no overwriting, which reflects the tighter editorial judgment I tend toward for a "brief" condolence note. Response B leans on heavier, more stacked emotional formulations ("losing a member of the family," "they were lucky to have someone who loved them so much") that read as the slightly more effusive, additive style of a mid-tier draft. The cleaner economy and tone-matching of A make me moderately confident it is mine.
